description: Benu is San Francisco's most decorated three-Michelin-starred restaurant and one of the great dining destinations in North America — a serene, oasis-like tasting menu experience in a 1922 SoMa building from James Beard Award-winning chef Corey Lee, formerly chef de cuisine at The French Laundry. Lee's singular genius lies in his marriage of Korean and Chinese culinary traditions with the finest California ingredients, producing dishes of extraordinary refinement: faux shark's fin and xiao long bao, barbecued quail with house-made XO sauce, an elevated Korean beef barbecue. Benu entered the World's 50 Best list in 2019 and has continuously held three Michelin stars since 2014.
highlights: Three Michelin stars since 2014; World's 50 Best; chef Corey Lee; Korean-Chinese-Californian tasting menu; 1922 SoMa building; James Beard Award; NY Times restaurant worth a plane ride.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; World's 50 Best; James Beard Foundation; La Liste; Condé Nast Traveler; New York Times

description: Quince is one of the most beautiful and complete fine dining experiences in America — a three-Michelin-starred Jackson Square restaurant from chef Michael Tusk and Lindsay Tusk, where a contemporary California-Italian menu inspired by an exclusive partnership with Fresh Run Farm in Bolinas changes nightly and is served in a gloriously restored early-20th-century building of exposed brick, warm wood, and candlelight. New in 2025 is the Bolinas Bar, offering a five-course California Coast and Valleys menu, while the main dining room delivers a full eight-to-ten course gastronomy tasting. The wine programme is among the very finest in San Francisco.
highlights: Three Michelin stars; Jackson Square historic building; exclusive Fresh Run Farm partnership; chef Michael Tusk; nightly-changing tasting menu and new Bolinas Bar; La Liste ranked; exceptional wine programme.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; La Liste; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out San Francisco; Eater SF

description: Atelier Crenn is the most poetic three-Michelin-starred restaurant in America — the intimate Cow Hollow atelier of chef Dominique Crenn, the first woman in the United States to earn three Michelin stars, where each tasting menu is structured as a poem and the pescatarian cuisine meditates on her Breton heritage and California present. Sourcing from her partner Bleu Belle Farm in Sonoma, Crenn weaves sea urchin custard, impeccable seafood preparations, and a brioche from her grandmother's recipe into one of the most emotionally resonant dining experiences in the world. Green Star-recognised for its commitment to regenerative agriculture and ethical sourcing.
highlights: Three Michelin stars; first woman to earn three Michelin stars in US; chef Dominique Crenn; Cow Hollow; pescatarian menu as poem; Bleu Belle Farm; two Green Stars; James Beard Award.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; La Liste; James Beard Foundation; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out SF

description: Californios is America's most decorated Mexican fine dining restaurant — a two-Michelin-starred SoMa destination from chef Val M. Cantú that was ranked No. 14 on North America's 50 Best Restaurants 2025 and was the first in the world to earn two Michelin stars for Mexican cuisine. In a dramatic room of black walls, candlelight, and vibrant art, Cantú's three-hour tasting menu applies the rigour of the finest European kitchens to the depth of Mexico's culinary traditions, with seasonal California ingredients providing the palette. Dishes like lime-cured cold-smoked hamachi with sweet lime aguachile and Buddha's hand represent a cooking intelligence that has made Cantú one of the world's great chef-storytellers.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; North America's 50 Best #14 2025; first Mexican restaurant worldwide to earn two Michelin stars; chef Val M. Cantú; SoMa; three-hour tasting menu; black walls and vibrant art.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; North America's 50 Best 2025; La Liste; Condé Nast Traveler; Eater SF; Time Out SF

description: Lazy Bear is San Francisco's most beloved two-Michelin-starred dinner party — a North America's 50 Best-listed Mission District restaurant from chef David Barzelay where guests book 'tickets' to a hyper-seasonal modern American tasting menu served across a 2.5-hour evening in a swanky bi-level warehouse styled like a mogul's hunting lodge. The menu changes with every service, the cultured butter and whipped scrambled eggs are legendary constants, and a beverage programme includes an optional private wine tasting in the 'bear cave.' Resy named it one of the ten defining San Francisco restaurants of 2025.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; North America's 50 Best; chef David Barzelay; Mission District; dinner party ticket format; bi-level warehouse dining room; Resy top SF restaurant 2025; La Liste ranked.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; North America's 50 Best; La Liste; Resy; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out SF

description: Saison is one of San Francisco's most elemental two-Michelin-starred restaurants — a SoMa warehouse destination where an open wood-burning hearth anchors both the kitchen and the spirit of the menu, and where chef Joshua Skenes creates a deeply seasonal tasting menu of extraordinary intensity, sourcing exclusively from the finest Northern California farms and fishermen. The dark, moody interior, live fire cookery, and singular focus make Saison one of the city's most visceral and atmospheric fine dining experiences. The adjoining Saison Cellar + Wine Bar, opened in 2024 with sommelier Mark Bright, further deepens the experience.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; SoMa warehouse with open hearth; chef Joshua Skenes; wood-fire-driven tasting menu; dark moody interior; La Liste ranked; Saison Cellar wine bar adjacent.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; La Liste; Eater SF; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out SF

description: Birdsong is one of San Francisco's most critically admired two-Michelin-starred restaurants — a SoMa destination from chef Christopher Bleidorn where heritage cooking, whole-animal butchery, and live-fire technique are combined in a high-ceilinged, elegantly appointed dining room. Two tasting menus — a 2.5-hour Discovery and a 3.5-hour Journey — celebrate the Pacific Coast's seasonal larder with a deft touch that balances the rugged appeal of flame-kissed meats with delicacy and whimsy. The restaurant's whole-produce philosophy and community ethos make it one of the most ethically considered destinations in the city.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; chef Christopher Bleidorn; SoMa; live-fire heritage cooking; whole-animal butchery; two tasting menu formats including 3.5-hour Journey; high-ceilinged elegant dining room.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; Time Out San Francisco; Condé Nast Traveler; OpenTable

description: Kiln is San Francisco's most compelling rising star — a two-Michelin-starred Hayes Valley restaurant from chef John Wesley and general manager Julianna Yang that opened in May 2023, earned its first star in 2024, and was elevated to two stars in 2025, one of the fastest ascents in the city's history. The 34-seat former garage — with a 30-foot ceiling, exposed concrete walls, and a Sonoma olive tree growing from a custom serving station — hosts a 20-course tasting menu that blends French, Japanese, and Scandinavian techniques in service of an uncompromising study in intention, simplicity, and purity of flavour. Wesley won the 2024 Michelin Young Chef Award.
highlights: Two Michelin stars (elevated from one in just one year); chef John Wesley; 2024 Michelin Young Chef Award; Hayes Valley former garage; 20-course tasting menu; 30-foot ceiling with Sonoma olive tree.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; Time Out SF; Resy; Modern Luxury

description: Acquerello is San Francisco's most treasured Italian fine dining institution — a two-Michelin-starred Nob Hill restaurant in a converted former church, open since 1989 and operated by chef Suzette Gresham and Giancarlo Paterlini, where one of the country's great Italian wine cellars supports a seasonal tasting menu of exceptional depth and artistry. The Wine Spectator Grand Award, uninterrupted since 2012, reflects a cellar of extraordinary breadth, and the arrival of the legendary mignardises trolley signals the perfect close to a meal. Acquerello is the kind of great American restaurant that reminds you why fine dining institutions matter.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; former church Nob Hill setting; open since 1989; chef Suzette Gresham; Wine Spectator Grand Award since 2012; one of the best Italian wine cellars in the US; legendary mignardises trolley.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; La Liste; Wine Spectator; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out SF

description: Sons & Daughters is one of San Francisco's most consistently admired two-Michelin-starred restaurants — a New Nordic-influenced tasting menu destination from chef Harrison Cheney, where ceramics made in collaboration with Oakland ceramicist Erin Hupp complement a seasonal menu of extraordinary visual and culinary precision, cooked in an open kitchen that plays an active role in the experience. The restaurant relocated to Hayes Valley in late 2025, bringing its acclaimed formula to a new home. Green Star-recognised; Cheney won the 2023 Michelin Young Chef Award.
highlights: Two Michelin stars; Green Star; chef Harrison Cheney; 2023 Michelin Young Chef Award; bespoke ceramics from Oakland maker; New Nordic-influenced; open kitchen; relocated to Hayes Valley 2025.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room with open kitchen
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; Resy; Time Out SF; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Restaurant Gary Danko is San Francisco's most enduring fine dining institution — a Michelin-starred Fisherman's Wharf landmark open since 1999 that combines classical French technique with Californian sensibility in a warm, flower-adorned dining room of unhurried luxury. Relais & Châteaux, Wine Spectator Grand Award-winning since 2001, AAA Five Diamond-rated, and recipient of multiple James Beard Awards, Gary Danko represents the finest tradition of great American special-occasion restaurants. The cheese trolley with over 20 selections, glazed oysters with osetra caviar, and impeccably suited servers complete an experience that continues to evolve after 25 years.
highlights: One Michelin star; Relais & Châteaux; Wine Spectator Grand Award since 2001; AAA Five Diamond; Fisherman's Wharf institution since 1999; multiple James Beard Awards; 20+ selection cheese trolley.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; La Liste; Wine Spectator; Relais & Châteaux; James Beard Foundation; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Angler is the most scenically positioned fine dining restaurant in San Francisco — a one-Michelin-starred waterfront destination on the Embarcadero from Saison Hospitality, where an expansive wood-burning hearth presides over a menu of exceptional line-caught and hand-dived seafood, with views of the Bay and the Bay Bridge. Chef Joe Hou leads a kitchen where every dish is touched by fire and intended for sharing, with the raw bar's daily oyster selection and live fire treatment of whole fish and crustaceans making this one of the most exciting seafood restaurants in California. Named one of Esquire and GQ's best new restaurants at opening.
highlights: One Michelin star; Embarcadero waterfront with Bay Bridge views; Saison Hospitality; wood-burning hearth; hand-dived and line-caught seafood; Esquire and GQ Best New Restaurant; private Game Room.
restaurant_setting: Waterfront dining room with Bay views
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Esquire; GQ; Eater SF; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out SF

description: Niku Steakhouse is San Francisco's most ambitious and celebrated luxury steakhouse — a one-Michelin-starred Design District restaurant from the Omakase Restaurant Group, where an in-house dry-aging programme, binchotan charcoal, and wood-fired grill are deployed in service of Japanese A5 wagyu, premium domestic cuts, and Northern California foraged ingredients. Executive chef Dustin Falcon (formerly of Lazy Bear and The French Laundry) leads a counter-centred dining room where guests watch each preparation unfold, combining American steakhouse tradition with Japanese culinary precision.
highlights: One Michelin star; Design District; chef Dustin Falcon (French Laundry alum); in-house dry-aging; Japanese A5 wagyu; binchotan charcoal and wood fire; Omakase Restaurant Group; chef's counter.
restaurant_setting: Design-led dining room with chef's counter
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; OpenTable; San Francisco Travel; Time Out SF

description: Mister Jiu's is one of the most important restaurants in America — a one-Michelin-starred Chinatown landmark from James Beard Award-winning chef Brandon Jew, housed in the historic former Four Seas banquet hall on Grant Avenue in the nation's oldest Chinatown. Jew's contemporary Chinese-American menu is simultaneously a celebration, a preservation, and a reinvention of the culinary traditions of San Francisco's oldest neighbourhood, with his Peking-style roast duck as the centrepiece of a communal banquet menu named a defining SF restaurant of 2025 by Resy. The room itself — a gloriously atmospheric double-height former banquet hall — is one of the great dining settings in the city.
highlights: One Michelin star; James Beard Award; historic Four Seas Chinatown building; chef Brandon Jew; contemporary Chinese-American; communal banquet with Peking duck; Resy defining SF restaurant 2025.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Resy; James Beard Foundation; Condé Nast Traveler; Eater SF; Time Out SF

description: Nari is San Francisco's most acclaimed Thai fine dining restaurant — a one-Michelin-starred destination at the iconic Hotel Kabuki in Japantown, led by celebrated chef Pim Techamuanvivit, whose precision and creativity have re-defined how Thai cuisine can be experienced at the highest level. The dramatic setting within one of San Francisco's most storied design hotels — with its Japanese aesthetic and sophisticated palette — provides an ideal backdrop for Techamuanvivit's laser-focused cooking. Named one of GQ's Best New Restaurants and the NY Times' 50 Most Exciting US Restaurants.
highlights: One Michelin star; chef Pim Techamuanvivit; Hotel Kabuki Japantown; contemporary heritage Thai cuisine; GQ Best New Restaurant; NY Times 50 Most Exciting US Restaurants 2021; James Beard recognised.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; GQ; New York Times; James Beard Foundation; Eater SF; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Nisei is San Francisco's most personal and original one-Michelin-starred fine dining experience — a Russian Hill restaurant from chef David Yoshimura (2022 Michelin Young Chef Award) whose deeply autobiographical tasting menu of 'California Washoku' cuisine explores what it means to be Japanese American through a Michelin-level cooking intelligence honed at Californios. Washoku — harmony of food, balance of colour, preparation, and respect — is expressed in compositions of extraordinary sensitivity, using Northern California's finest ingredients through the lens of Japan's most refined culinary traditions.
highlights: One Michelin star; 2022 Michelin Young Chef Award; chef David Yoshimura; Russian Hill; California Washoku cuisine; deeply personal Japanese-American culinary identity; Californios alumnus.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; San Francisco Chronicle; Time Out SF

description: State Bird Provisions is one of San Francisco's most original Michelin-starred restaurants — a one-star Fillmore Street destination from chef-owners Stuart Brioza and Nicole Krasinski where an urban-rustic dining room hosts a perpetually evolving Asian-inflected California menu served dim-sum style from rolling carts and announced by servers in a communal, electric atmosphere. The eponymous State Bird — a crispy, umami-rich quail — remains one of the city's most celebrated dishes, and the combination of casual warmth, culinary ambition, and social energy makes this the quintessential non-formal San Francisco luxury dining experience.
highlights: One Michelin star; chefs Stuart Brioza and Nicole Krasinski; Fillmore; dim-sum cart service; Asian-inflected California cuisine; State Bird quail signature dish; quintessential SF dining atmosphere.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; Condé Nast Traveler; Resy; Time Out SF

description: Cotogna is the most lovable restaurant in San Francisco — a Michelin Selected Jackson Square Italian trattoria from three-star chef Michael Tusk and Lindsay Tusk that has been one of the most consistently excellent and sought-after tables in the city since 2011, with a daily-changing menu driven by the same Fresh Run Farm in Bolinas that supplies Quince next door, cooked in a wood-burning oven and on the spit with rustic generosity and depth of flavour rarely found outside Italy. Socialites, celebrities, and food critics consider it their neighbourhood restaurant: exposed brickwork, a gleaming copper bar, and warm wood tables create one of the most inviting interiors in the city.
highlights: Michelin Selected; Jackson Square; chef Michael Tusk (three-star Quince next door); wood-burning oven; Fresh Run Farm; daily-changing Italian menu; copper bar and brick interior; beloved by SF food elite.
restaurant_setting: Historic brick interior with copper bar
sources: Michelin Guide (Selected); Condé Nast Traveler; Eater SF; Resy; Time Out SF; Modern Luxury

description: O' by Claude Le Tohic is one of San Francisco's most technically formidable Michelin-starred restaurants — a one-star SoMa destination from French chef Claude Le Tohic, a Robuchon-trained former chef de cuisine of Joël Robuchon Las Vegas with multiple James Beard nominations, whose intimate 24-seat counter delivers a tasting menu of extraordinary classical French precision applied to the finest California seasonal produce. The combination of French culinary rigour, California terroir, and a deeply personal chef-to-guest counter experience makes it one of the most complete European-influenced fine dining experiences in the Bay Area.
highlights: One Michelin star; chef Claude Le Tohic; Robuchon-trained; 24-seat counter; SoMa; classical French technique with California produce; multiple James Beard nominations; intimate counter setting.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; San Francisco Chronicle; Time Out SF

description: Le Comptoir at Bar Crenn is the most intimate and music-forward expression of Dominique Crenn's culinary world — a one-Michelin-starred bar dining experience in Cow Hollow where a special tasting menu is available in the lounge alongside live jazz and curated wines, blending the sophistication of French fine dining with the warmth of a great wine bar. One of San Francisco's most distinctive combinations of musical programming, impeccable hospitality, and high-calibre cuisine — an atmosphere unlike anything else in the city.
highlights: One Michelin star; Dominique Crenn; Bar Crenn Cow Hollow lounge; tasting menu with live jazz; unique wine bar and fine dining hybrid; music-forward luxury experience.
restaurant_setting: Luxury wine bar with live music
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; Time Out SF; Cozymeal

description: Aphotic is San Francisco's most thrillingly original new seafood fine dining restaurant — a one-Michelin-starred SoMa destination that won the 2024 Michelin Exceptional Cocktails Award for its boundary-pushing beverage programme alongside a seafood-centred menu that brings the wild, briny depths of the Pacific Coast to the table with extraordinary skill and creative ambition. The kitchen's commitment to local, line-caught, and responsibly harvested seafood — combined with an exceptional cocktail programme — makes Aphotic one of the most complete and distinctive Michelin dining experiences to open in San Francisco in years.
highlights: One Michelin star; 2024 Michelin Exceptional Cocktails Award; SoMa; Pacific Coast sustainable seafood; extraordinary cocktail programme; one of the most original recent Michelin openings in SF.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; San Francisco Chronicle; Time Out SF

description: 7 Adams is one of the most talked-about additions to San Francisco's Michelin family — a one-star restaurant from chefs David Fisher and Serena Chow Fisher praised by Michelin for its 'signature magic': a deeply personal, artfully composed tasting menu drawn from the chefs' combined heritage and the extraordinary produce of the Bay Area, served in an intimate room that feels simultaneously personal and ambitious. The restaurant epitomises the independent, chef-driven spirit that makes San Francisco's fine dining culture unique.
highlights: One Michelin star; chefs David Fisher and Serena Chow Fisher; praised for 'signature magic'; deeply personal tasting menu; Bay Area produce-driven; one of the most admired recent SF debuts.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; San Francisco Chronicle; Resy

description: Sorrel is one of San Francisco's most celebrated neighbourhood fine dining restaurants — a one-Michelin-starred Pacific Heights destination from chef Alexander Hong that channels the flavours of the Italian and Mediterranean kitchen through a California-first lens, sourcing from the city's farmers' markets and expressing a cooking intelligence shaped by stages at restaurants including Quince. The warm, intimate dining room and approachable price point relative to SF's major tasting menu establishments make Sorrel one of the most compelling introductions to the city's Michelin world.
highlights: One Michelin star; chef Alexander Hong; Pacific Heights; Italian-Mediterranean Californian cuisine; farmers' market-driven; Quince alumnus; approachable price point; beloved neighbourhood star.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; San Francisco Chronicle; Time Out SF

description: Omakase is one of San Francisco's most acclaimed traditional Japanese fine dining experiences — a one-Michelin-starred SoMa counter offering a rigorously traditional Edomae-style nigiri experience, sourcing premium fish flown directly from Japan alongside the finest domestic products. The ten-seat counter format, meticulous sourcing, and exceptional shari (rice) precision make this one of the city's most technically exacting Japanese dining destinations, drawing comparison with the finest omakase restaurants in New York.
highlights: One Michelin star; SoMa; traditional Edomae nigiri omakase; premium fish flown from Japan; ten-seat counter; among the most technically precise Japanese dining in California.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; Time Out SF; Condé Nast Traveler

description: Hilda and Jesse is one of San Francisco's most original and warmly celebrated Michelin-starred restaurants — a one-star North Beach destination from chefs Kristina Liedags Compton and Rachel Sillcocks, who won the 2024 Michelin Outstanding Service Award, that delivers a 'total riot of flavors mixed with stellar ingredients' in one of the city's most joyful and personality-rich dining rooms. Opening in late 2021 and earning a first star in 2024, Hilda and Jesse reflects a queer-owned, community-rooted San Francisco dining culture at its very best: technically serious, emotionally generous, and absolutely individual.
highlights: One Michelin star; 2024 Michelin Outstanding Service Award; chefs Kristina Liedags Compton and Rachel Sillcocks; North Beach; 'total riot of flavors'; queer-owned; joyful and personality-rich dining room.
sources: Michelin Guide California 2025; Eater SF; San Francisco Chronicle; Resy; Time Out SF
